---
pagetitle: "Discord - Architecture 2.0"
description: "Placeholder community page for the Architecture 2.0 Discord server."
format:
  html:
    page-layout: custom
    toc: false
---

```{=html}
<div class="site-wrap site-narrow">
<header class="site-masthead">
  <span class="pill">Architecture 2.0</span>
  <span class="pill hot">Community</span>
</header>

<section class="site-hero">
  <div class="hub-kicker">Discord</div>
  <h1>Community chat</h1>
  <p class="subtitle">A lightweight place for questions, announcements, reading groups, and workshop coordination</p>
</section>

<section class="community-panel" id="invite">
  <div>
    <span class="site-note-label">Invite link pending</span>
    <h2>Discord server coming soon</h2>
    <p>
      We recommend Discord as the real-time community layer for Architecture 2.0.
      GitHub Discussions remains the durable place for proposals, decisions,
      submission questions, and anything that should be citable later.
    </p>
  </div>
  <nav class="site-actions" aria-label="Community actions">
    <a class="site-action disabled" href="#invite" aria-disabled="true">Invite coming soon</a>
    <a class="site-action secondary" href="https://github.com/harvard-edge/arch2/discussions">Use GitHub Discussions</a>
  </nav>
</section>

<section class="site-section">
  <div class="section-heading">
    <h2>Suggested channels</h2>
    <p>These are placeholders for the initial server shape.</p>
  </div>
  <div class="site-strands cols-2">
    <div class="strand">
      <span class="site-pill">Start</span>
      <h3>#introductions</h3>
      <p>Who you are, what you work on, and whether you are reading, submitting, teaching, or building.</p>
    </div>
    <div class="strand">
      <span class="site-pill">Ask</span>
      <h3>#questions</h3>
      <p>Fast help for newcomers trying to understand the book, tools, datasets, and workshops.</p>
    </div>
    <div class="strand">
      <span class="site-pill">Share</span>
      <h3>#papers-and-posts</h3>
      <p>New papers, SIGARCH posts, talks, and reading-list nominations.</p>
    </div>
    <div class="strand">
      <span class="site-pill">Submit</span>
      <h3>#workshops-and-cfps</h3>
      <p>Calls for papers, tutorial proposals, artifact demos, and submission coordination.</p>
    </div>
  </div>
</section>

<section class="provenance">
  Discord is for lightweight conversation. Use <a href="https://github.com/harvard-edge/arch2/discussions">GitHub Discussions</a>
  when an idea should be searchable, reviewable, or linked from a pull request.
</section>
</div>
```
